question: Why is watch() without arguments bad for performance?

options.text: It makes an HTTP request on every change
options.explanation: watch() does not make HTTP requests — the problem is re-renders

options.text: It subscribes to all fields and causes a full component re-render on any change

options.explanation: watch() does not block the thread — the problem is excessive re-renders

options.text: It consumes a lot of memory
options.explanation: The main issue is not memory, but the number of re-renders

question: How does useWatch differ from watch?

options.text: useWatch subscribes only to the specified fields
useWatch can be used in child components via control

options.explanation: useWatch does cause re-renders, but only in the component where it is used and only when the specified fields change

options.text: useWatch isolates re-renders from the rest of the form

question: Which approach helps isolate re-renders in large forms?

options.text: Use watch() for all fields along with shouldComponentUpdate
options.explanation: watch() subscribes to everything — this is the opposite of isolation

options.text: Extract parts of the form into separate components with memo and useWatch

options.explanation: useCallback memoizes functions but does not prevent re-renders caused by watch()

options.text: Increase the debounce delay for onChange
options.explanation: Debounce reduces frequency but does not isolate re-renders
